user_query:
I’m a youth cricket coach who uses training photos and slow‑motion clips for technical feedback. Please turn the attached notes into clear, tone‑appropriate communications and add a simple validation so I can reuse this workflow weekly.

Inputs (provided in workspace):
- input/analysis_notes.csv — photo-driven coaching notes by player and focus area
- input/verbose_message.txt — an overly long session invite that needs two tone rewrites
- input/policy_points.md — key policy points for parents about training photos

Deliverables and constraints:
1) Weekly status summary (compose):
   - Create output/report/weekly_performance_summary.md that summarizes input/analysis_notes.csv.
   - Include a title containing the exact date range from the data (minimum and maximum session_date).
   - Include these computable metrics:
     - Total players analyzed: distinct player count from the CSV
     - Total sessions: total rows in the CSV
   - Add a section labeled "Focus areas (top 3):" listing exactly three lines of the form "AREA_NAME - COUNT" for the top three focus areas by frequency in the CSV (order may be any; counts must be correct).
   - Add a brief paragraph (≤120 words) explaining how photos informed the coaching points.

2) Two rewrites of a session invite (rewrite for tone, clarity, brevity):
   - Read input/verbose_message.txt and produce two versions:
     - output/messages/review_invite_friendly.txt (80–120 words) — use a warm, encouraging tone and include the word "please" and a friendly sign-off like "Thanks" or "Thank you".
     - output/messages/review_invite_assertive.txt (80–120 words) — use a firm, action‑oriented tone and include the word "must".
   - Both rewrites must preserve these exact substrings from the original message: "Saturday 10:00 AM, 2026-05-02", "Indoor nets, Hall B", and "RSVP by 2026-05-01 17:00".

3) Draft an email to parents (draft email):
   - Create output/emails/parents_photo_workflow_email.txt.
   - First line must be a subject line starting with "Subject: " (e.g., "Subject: Introducing our photo feedback process").
   - Include two short paragraphs describing the value of photos in coaching and the workflow, followed by a bullet list (4–6 items) derived from input/policy_points.md.
   - The body must include the exact deadline date string "2026-05-01" and the contact email from the policy file.
   - End the email with a sign-off line containing "— Coach Alex".

4) Automated validation (test writing and validation):
   - Write tests/validate_outputs.py (standard library only). When run, it must:
     - Parse input/analysis_notes.csv to compute: distinct player count, total rows, top 3 focus areas with counts, and min/max dates; verify weekly_performance_summary.md contains these values and the three top focus areas with correct counts.
     - Check the two rewritten invite files each have 80–120 words; both contain the exact substrings "Saturday 10:00 AM, 2026-05-02", "Indoor nets, Hall B", and "RSVP by 2026-05-01 17:00"; the friendly version contains "please" and either "Thanks" or "Thank you"; the assertive version contains "must".
     - Check the parent email file has a first line starting with "Subject:", contains the deadline "2026-05-01" and "coach.alex@example.com", has between 4 and 6 bullet lines (lines beginning with "- "), and ends with a line containing "— Coach Alex".
   - The script must write a machine-readable report to output/test_report.json with pass/fail for each check and a brief message per item, and exit non‑zero if any check fails.

Please ensure all specified output paths are created. Do not use external services or libraries.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
